tax_rules.scope: All supplies of goods and services

jurisdiction_rules.prompt_text:
Korean Accounting Practice (K-IFRS)

The following rules reflect Korean accounting conventions that differ from general IFRS practice. Apply these when the entity's jurisdiction is Korea.

1. Payable Classification
매입채무 (Trade payables) is reserved strictly for purchases of inventory and merchandise from suppliers. All other payables — including fixed asset purchases, service fees, non-trade promissory notes, and any obligation not arising from inventory procurement — use 미지급금 (Other payables). This includes promissory notes issued to sellers for asset purchases.
미지급비용 (Accrued expenses) is used for time-based accruals (interest, wages earned but not yet invoiced). Distinguished from 미지급금 which is for specific invoiced or determinable amounts.

2. Current / Non-Current Split at Initial Recognition
When a payable involves installments or deferred payment terms, split it at the time of the transaction into:
  - 미지급금: portion due within 12 months from the transaction date
  - 장기미지급금: portion due beyond 12 months
Calculate the present value of each portion separately using the stated or market discount rate. Do not record as a single long-term payable.

3. Manufacturing Cost Accounting
Factory overhead costs — including factory electricity, factory rent, production staff meals, and other production-related indirect costs — are recorded as 제조경비 (Manufacturing overhead), not capitalized directly into 재공품 (Work in process) or inventory. Production labor uses 노무비 (Direct labor) and production materials use 재료비 (Direct materials) as distinct cost accounts.

4. Share Cancellation
When shares are repurchased and cancelled above par value, the entire excess is recorded as 감자차손 (Capital reduction loss) under 자본조정 (Capital adjustments). Do not allocate to 이익잉여금 (Retained earnings) or 주식발행초과금 (Share premium), regardless of original issuance price. This is not an ambiguity — do not ask for clarification.
